name: Ouster OS1 — Mid-Range Lidar
description: Digital lidar with 5.2M points/sec, 240m range, 360° FOV. 447g, IP68/IP69K rated. REV7 sensor with up to 128 channels. NDAA compliant, US manufactured. Standard for UAS mapping and autonomous navigation.
ndaa_note: Ouster Inc., San Francisco CA (merged with Velodyne). US LiDAR (NYSE: OUST). NDAA compliant.

name: Ouster OS0 — Short-Range Wide-FOV Lidar
description: Ultra-wide 90° vertical FOV lidar optimized for close-range perception and robotics. Same digital architecture as OS1. Ideal for obstacle avoidance and indoor navigation on UAS.
ndaa_note: Ouster Inc., San Francisco CA (merged with Velodyne). US LiDAR (NYSE: OUST). NDAA compliant.

name: Ouster OS2 — Long-Range Lidar
description: Long-range variant optimized for 200+ meter detection. Narrow vertical FOV focused on distant object detection. Suited for infrastructure inspection, corridor mapping, and perimeter security from UAS.
ndaa_note: Ouster Inc., San Francisco CA (merged with Velodyne). US LiDAR (NYSE: OUST). NDAA compliant.

name: RIEGL miniVUX-1UAV — Survey-Grade Airborne Lidar
description: Survey-grade airborne laser scanner at 1.55 kg. 100 kHz pulse rate, 330m range, eye-safe Class 1. Designed for UAV-based topographic mapping with centimeter accuracy. Austrian manufacture, NATO-aligned.
ndaa_note: RIEGL Laser Measurement Systems, Horn, Austria. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: RIEGL VUX-120²³ — High-Performance Airborne Scanner
description: High-performance airborne laser scanner at 2 kg with 1.8 MHz effective measurement rate, 720m operating altitude, 10mm accuracy. Multi-target echo processing. Professional survey and mapping standard.
ndaa_note: RIEGL Laser Measurement Systems, Horn, Austria. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: YellowScan Voyager — Integrated Lidar+INS
description: Integrated lidar mapping solution combining RIEGL scanner with INS and processing software. 2M usable points/sec, 1cm accuracy. Turnkey solution for professional UAV surveying.
ndaa_note: YellowScan, Montpellier, France. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: Livox Mid-360 — Budget Lidar
description: Budget 360° lidar at only 265g and $600-900. Non-repetitive scanning pattern provides high point density over time. Strong price-to-performance ratio but Chinese manufacture — NOT NDAA compliant.

name: Ainstein US-D1 — Radar Altimeter
description: 24 GHz radar altimeter with 50m range, ±6cm accuracy, 100 Hz update rate. 110g, IP67. UART/CAN interface. NDAA compliant, ITAR-free. Standard radar altimeter for ArduPilot/PX4 platforms. US manufactured.
ndaa_note: Ainstein Inc., Lawrence KS. US radar altimeter manufacturer. NDAA compliant.

name: Echodyne EchoGuard — Metamaterial ESA Radar
description: Metamaterial electronically scanned array (ESA) radar that detects DJI Phantom-4 class drones at ~1 km and vehicles at 3.5 km. 1.25 kg, 120°x80° FOV, under 50W. No mechanical scanning parts. NDAA compliant. Used for airspace awareness and counter-UAS detection.
ndaa_note: Echodyne Corp, Kirkland WA. US metamaterial radar. NDAA compliant. Backed by Gates Ventures/Vulcan.

name: Echodyne EchoGuard CR — 360° Radar
description: 360° coverage variant of EchoGuard using multiple metamaterial ESA panels. Provides full-hemisphere drone detection and tracking for persistent airspace monitoring.
ndaa_note: Echodyne Corp, Kirkland WA. US metamaterial radar. NDAA compliant. Backed by Gates Ventures/Vulcan.

name: Advanced Navigation Motus — MEMS IMU
description: Breakthrough MEMS IMU achieving 0.2°/hr gyro bias instability — approaching FOG-grade performance from MEMS technology. Only 26g. Massive cost and weight reduction vs fiber-optic gyros for defense-grade navigation.
ndaa_note: Advanced Navigation, Sydney, Australia. Allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: SBG Systems Quanta Micro — Tactical INS
description: Compact tactical-grade INS at approximately 50g with 0.8°/hr gyro bias. Integrates GNSS receiver for full navigation solution. French manufacture — EU/NATO sovereign supply chain.
ndaa_note: SBG Systems, Rueil-Malmaison, France. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: SBG Systems Pulse-40 — Ultra-Miniature INS
description: Ultra-miniature tactical-grade INS at only 12g and 0.3W. Designed for micro-UAS and precision-guided munitions where SWaP is extreme. Includes GNSS and dual-antenna heading.
ndaa_note: SBG Systems, Rueil-Malmaison, France. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: SBG Systems Ellipse-D — Dual-Antenna INS/GNSS
description: Dual-antenna RTK INS/GNSS providing centimeter-level positioning and precise heading without magnetometer. Standard for UAS survey and mapping applications. French manufacture.
ndaa_note: SBG Systems, Rueil-Malmaison, France. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: VectorNav VN-100 — AHRS/IMU
description: Compact AHRS/IMU with under 10°/hr gyro bias in under 30g package. Industry standard for commercial UAS attitude reference. US manufactured, NDAA compliant.
ndaa_note: VectorNav Technologies, Dallas TX. US AHRS/INS manufacturer. NDAA compliant.

name: VectorNav VN-200 — GPS/INS
description: Integrated GPS/INS module providing position, velocity, and attitude. Kalman filter fuses MEMS IMU with GNSS. Mid-range solution between VN-100 AHRS and VN-300 dual-antenna.
ndaa_note: VectorNav Technologies, Dallas TX. US AHRS/INS manufacturer. NDAA compliant.

name: VectorNav VN-300 — Dual-Antenna GPS/INS
description: Dual-antenna GPS/INS providing precise heading and position without magnetometer. Highest-performance VectorNav solution for UAS and autonomous systems.
ndaa_note: VectorNav Technologies, Dallas TX. US AHRS/INS manufacturer. NDAA compliant.

name: Inertial Labs INS-D/P — Defense INS
description: Defense-grade INS systems for UAS platforms. INS-D for dual-antenna heading, INS-P for precision navigation. US manufactured with NDAA compliance. Supports PX4/ArduPilot integration.
ndaa_note: Inertial Labs Inc., Paeonian Springs VA. US GNSS/INS manufacturer. NDAA compliant. DoD supplier.

name: ARK Electronics Flow MR — Optical Flow Sensor
description: Mid-range optical flow sensor for GPS-denied positioning. Blue UAS Framework listed. MEMS IMU integrated (Analog Devices/Murata). Made in USA. Provides visual odometry for indoor and GNSS-denied flight.
ndaa_note: Designed and manufactured USA. NDAA compliant.

name: KVH 1750 IMU — FOG
description: Fiber-optic gyro IMU with under 0.05°/hr bias instability. Gold standard for high-accuracy navigation in defense UAS. Significantly heavier and more expensive than MEMS but provides gyro performance 10-100x better.
ndaa_note: KVH Industries, Middletown RI (NASDAQ: KVHI). US FOG IMU manufacturer. NDAA compliant.

name: MicaSense RedEdge-P — Multispectral Camera
description: 5-band multispectral camera plus 5.1 MP panchromatic for UAS precision agriculture and environmental monitoring. 300g. NDAA compliant, DIU approved. Industry standard for agricultural remote sensing.
ndaa_note: MicaSense, acquired by AgEagle Aerial Systems, Wichita KS. US manufacturer. NDAA compliant.

name: MicaSense Altum-PT — Multispectral + Thermal
description: Combined multispectral (5 bands) plus 320x256 thermal sensor for simultaneous crop stress and temperature mapping. Adds FLIR thermal to RedEdge capabilities. NDAA compliant.
ndaa_note: MicaSense, acquired by AgEagle Aerial Systems, Wichita KS. US manufacturer. NDAA compliant.

name: Sentera 6X — Multi-Sensor Camera
description: 6-sensor payload with 5x 3.2 MP multispectral cameras plus 20 MP RGB plus optional FLIR Boson thermal. 290g. NDAA compliant, DIU approved. Designed for precision agriculture and defense ISR on small UAS.
ndaa_note: Sentera, Minneapolis MN — acquired by John Deere May 2025. US manufacturer. NDAA compliant.

name: Livox Avia — Solid-State LiDAR (FAST-LIO reference)
description: Non-repetitive scanning solid-state LiDAR with 70° circular FoV. The reference sensor for FAST-LIO/FAST-LIVO SLAM stacks from HKU-MARS lab. Triple-return, 240K points/sec, integrated IMU. Used on DJI Matrice 300/350 and custom research quads.

name: Livox Mid-360 — Budget 360° LiDAR
description: 360° non-repetitive solid-state LiDAR at budget price point. Inertial-grade IMU, 200K pts/sec. Popular for GPS-denied indoor navigation builds. Compatible with FAST-LIO2, Point-LIO.

name: Livox Horizon — Wide-FoV Solid-State LiDAR
description: 81.7° x 25.1° FoV solid-state LiDAR, automotive-grade. Used in L4 autonomous driving and large drone mapping. FAST-LIO compatible.

name: RoboSense RS-Helios — 32-Channel Spinning LiDAR
description: 32-channel mechanical spinning LiDAR with 360° FoV. Used in S-FAST_LIO RS-LiDAR configuration (zlwang7). 70m range, compact form factor for drone integration.
ndaa_note: RoboSense (Suteng Innovation Technology), Shenzhen, China. Non-NDAA. Chinese LiDAR manufacturer.

name: Velodyne VLP-16 (Puck) — 16-Channel Spinning LiDAR
description: Industry-standard 16-channel spinning LiDAR. 100m range, 300K pts/sec. The most commonly used LiDAR in drone SLAM research. Supported by virtually all SLAM stacks.
ndaa_note: Velodyne Lidar merged with Ouster 2023. Ouster Inc., San Francisco CA (NYSE: OUST). US company. NDAA compliant.

name: Xsens MTi-30 — Tactical-Grade IMU
description: MEMS IMU used paired with RS-Helios in S-FAST_LIO RS-LiDAR datasets. 400Hz gyro/accel, AHRS output. Common external IMU for LiDAR-inertial SLAM when LiDAR lacks built-in IMU.
ndaa_note: Xsens/Movella, Enschede, Netherlands. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant.

name: Inertial Labs INS-B — GPS-Aided Inertial Navigation System (Basic)
description: Tactical-grade single-antenna GPS-aided INS. Ideal for LiDAR/optical camera UAV payloads. 0.1 deg dynamic heading accuracy, 1.5m horizontal position (GPS L1), 1cm+1ppm RTK. Supports NovAtel OEM719, u-blox ZED-F9P, or Septentrio mosaic-X5. IP67, MIL-STD-810G. Compatible with Pixhawk autopilot and COBHAM AVIATOR UAV 200. 'Tunnel Guide' feature: 0.2% DT position accuracy in GNSS-denied environments.

name: Inertial Labs INS-P — GPS-Aided INS with Fluxgate Compass (Professional)
description: Professional tactical-grade GPS-aided INS with embedded gyro-compensated Fluxgate compass — maintains heading without GNSS. 0.3 deg static heading accuracy, 0.1 deg dynamic. Key differentiator: free-inertial heading to <0.4 deg (3-sigma) in GPS-denied ops. Industrial-grade IMU variant of the same form factor. Supports Pixhawk. RESEPI LiDAR integration-ready.

name: Inertial Labs INS-D — Dual Antenna GPS-Aided INS
description: Dual-antenna GPS-aided INS for high-precision heading without magnetic compass. 0.15 deg heading accuracy at 1m baseline (NovAtel OEM7720) or 0.4 deg at 1m (dual u-blox ZED-F9P). 1cm RTK. Tactical-grade IMU. Used on ISR fixed-wing platforms and precision survey drones requiring heading independent of magnetic environment.

name: Inertial Labs AHRS-10P — MEMS Attitude & Heading Reference System
description: Compact MEMS AHRS with fluxgate magnetometers. 0.6 deg dynamic heading accuracy, 0.08 deg pitch/roll. 90 x 27 x 26mm, 84g. Interfaces: RS-232, RS-422, CAN. IP67. Designed for UAVs, gimbals, antenna pointing, and stabilization systems where full GPS-aided INS is overkill. ITAR-free.

name: Inertial Labs AHRS-II-P — Tactical-Grade Attitude & Heading Reference System
description: Tactical-grade AHRS with Inertial Labs 8mm fluxgate magnetometers — superior stability over MEMS magnetics. 0.6 deg gyro-stabilized heading, 0.08 deg pitch/roll dynamic accuracy over full temperature range. Kalman filter based algorithms for UAV, aircraft, vessels, ROV, UGV. IP67. Used as standalone orientation reference where GPS weight/cost is prohibitive.

name: Inertial Labs M-AJ-QUATRO — CRPA Anti-Jamming Antenna System
description: Advanced CRPA (Controlled Reception Pattern Antenna) anti-jamming system for Assured PNT in GPS-jammed/spoofed environments. Operates on all L1, L2, L5 GNSS bands. Adaptive digital nulling: >34dB interference suppression (export-free model). Compatible with all Inertial Labs INS products and commercial GNSS receivers — plug-and-play integration. Released March 2025 (post-VIAVI acquisition). Targets military UAVs, loitering munitions, ISR platforms, commercial aviation.

name: ARK Electronics ARK Flow
description: Blue UAS listed DroneCAN optical flow + distance sensor for GPS-denied flight. PAW3902 optical flow (tracks >9 lux, 80mm to infinity, 7.4 rad/s max), Broadcom AFBR-S50LV85D ToF LiDAR (30m range, 12.4°x6.2° FoV, 32px), ICM-42688-P IMU. STM32F412, 2x CAN JST-GH. 3x3x1.4cm, 5g. Open source firmware + ROS2 support. USA made.

compliance.note: Blue UAS Framework listed. NDAA compliant. Made in USA.

ndaa_note: ARK Electronics, USA. Designed and manufactured USA. NDAA compliant.

name: Draganfly Long Range LiDAR
description: Survey-grade 360° scanning LiDAR for UAV, helicopter, vehicle, and backpack deployment. Teledyne 40-year aerospace sensor heritage. Applanix (Trimble) IMU+GNSS. 750m range at 50kHz. 2M pts/sec. 4 laser returns for bare-earth under thick vegetation. 0.5cm point cloud precision, 2cm global accuracy. Includes pre-processing software (LAS/LAZ output). LiDAR360 post-processing available. Used for Ukraine demining ops.

compliance.note: North American manufactured. Teledyne sensor used in commercial and military aerospace.

sources.title: Draganfly Long Range LiDAR product page

name: Draganfly Vital Intelligence
description: Software platform turning any RGB camera into a contactless biometric monitoring system. Extracts heart rate, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation (SpO2), and body temperature from standard video feed — no specialized hardware. API available for telehealth integrations. Deployable on drones, robots, kiosks, handheld devices, and edge computing platforms. Anonymized data option. Dashboard for real-time display. Deployed in corporate offices, correctional facilities, schools, restaurants, sheriff's offices across North America. COVID-era origin (symptom screening at scale).

specs.modality: RGB video (standard camera — no thermal required)
specs.chief_scientist: Prof. Javaan Chahl, Univ. of South Australia / Defense Science and Technology Chair

compliance.note: Software product. North American developed. No personal data registered.

name: Gremsy T3V3 Gimbal
description: 3-axis MAVLink-compatible gimbal stabilizer for small-medium UAS payloads.
source: Frontier Precision / Gremsy dealer price list 2022

name: Gremsy T7 Gimbal
description: 3-axis MAVLink-compatible gimbal stabilizer for heavier UAS payloads.
source: Frontier Precision / Gremsy dealer price list 2022

name: Gremsy Pixy U Gimbal
description: 3-axis gimbal stabilizer for compact UAS.
source: Frontier Precision / Gremsy dealer price list 2022

name: Gremsy Pixy WP Gimbal
description: 3-axis weatherproof gimbal stabilizer for outdoor/harsh-environment UAS operations.
source: Frontier Precision / Gremsy dealer price list 2022

name: GVI LiAir X3 LiDAR Mapping System
description: Integrated LiDAR + INS + 26MP camera for UAS mapping. 240k pts/s, AirPilot L3 autonomous flight, powerline inspection. Compatible with DJI M300 RTK.
source: Frontier Precision / GVI LiAir X3 datasheet 2022
